name: dplug.canvas.gradient
comment:
Implement a gradient object, that holds a LUT.
    Copyright: Chris Jones 2020.
    Copyright: Guillaume Piolat 2018-2025.
    License:   http://www.boost.org/LICENSE_1_0.txt

members.name: Gradient
members.comment:
Gradient class.

    Nothing here is a public API, it's only internal to
    dplug:canvas.

    A gradient owns:

      - a list of colours and positions (known as stops)
        along a single dimension from 0 to 1.
        The colors are a 32-bit RGBA quadruplet.

      - a look-up table of colors (LUT)
        indexed by blitters.

    It has a lookup table for the rasterizer, built lazily
    (was originally always 256 samples).

    FUTURE: size of look-up is dynamically choosen

members.name: addStop
members.comment: Add a color stop.

members.name: reset
members.comment:
Reset things, have zero color stop.
        Invalidate look-up.

members.name: length
members.comment: Returns: Numnber of color stops in this gradient.

members.name: lutLength
members.comment:
Get look-up length.
        This is invalidated in cases the color stops are
        redone with `.reset` or `.addStop`.

members.name: getLookup
members.comment: Returns: Complete LUT, of size `lutLength()`.

members.name: hasChanged
members.comment:
Returns: `true` if the color stops have changed,
        and LUT would be recomputed at next `.getLookup()`.

members.name: isOpaque
members.comment: Returns: `true` if gradient fully opaque.

name: dplug.canvas
comment:
Fast 2D software renderer.

  See an example of a Canvas-enabled UIElement in:
      `dplug.flatwidgets.windowresizer.UIWindowResizer`

  This is a DPlug-specific rework of dg2d by Cerjones.
  https://github.com/cerjones/dg2d
  - removal of truetype functionnality (since covered by dplug:graphics)
  - nothrow @nogc
  - rework of the Canvas itself, to resemble more the HTML5 Canvas API
  - Blitter delegate made explicit with a userData pointer
  - added html color parsing
  - no alignment requirements
  - clipping is done with the ImageRef input
  - added a few blendmodes
  However a failure of this fork is that for transforms and stroke() support
  you do need path abstraction in the end.

  dplug:canvas is pretty fast and writes 4 pixels at once.

  Bug: you can't use it on a widget that is full-size in your plugin.

  Copyright: Copyright Chris Jones 2020.
  Copyright: Copyright Guillaume Piolat 2020.
  License:   $(LINK2 http://www.boost.org/LICENSE_1_0.txt, Boost License 1.0)

And traverse pixels only once.

members.name: Canvas
members.comment:
A 2D Canvas able to render complex pathes into an `ImageRef!RGBA` buffer.
`Canvas` tries to follow loosely the HTML 5 Canvas API.

See_also: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLCanvasElement

Important:
    * Only works on RGBA output.
    * There need at least 12 extra bytes between lines (`trailingSamples = 3` in `OwnedImage`).
      You can use OwnedImage to have that guarantee. See https://github.com/AuburnSounds/Dplug/issues/563
      For now, avoid full-UI controls that use a Canvas.

Angular Gradient.
Specify the orientation in terms of an ellipse, for that we need 3 points.
(x0,y0) is the center of the ellipse
(x1,y1) is radius at 0 degrees
(x2,y2) is radius at 90 degrees
The radii don't need to be at right angles, so it can handle
ellipse that has been through any affine transform.

members.name: CanvasGradient
members.comment:
Holds both gradient data/table and positioning information.

You can create a gradient with:
- `createLinearGradient`,
- `createCircularGradient`,
- `createEllipticalGradient`,
- `createAngularGradient`,
every frame.
Then use `addColorStop` to set the color information.

The gradient data is managed by the `Canvas` object itself. All gradients are invalidated once
`initialize` has been called.

w
members.comment: Width of the meaningful area. Public in order to be an `Image`.

members.name: h
members.comment: Height of the meaningful area. Public in order to be an `Image`.

members.name: this
members.comment: Create empty and with no size.

members.name: this
members.comment:
Create with given initial size.

Params:
  w               = Width of meaningful area in pixels.
  h               = Height of meaningful area in pixels.
  border          = Number of border pixels around the meaningful area. For the right border, this could actually be more depending on other constraints.
  rowAlignment    = Alignment of the _first_ pixel of each row, in bytes (excluding border).
  xMultiplicity   = Starting with the first meaningful pixel of a line, force the number of addressable
                    pixels to be a multiple of `xMultiplicity`.
                    All these "padding" samples added at the right of each line if needed will be considered
                    part of the border and replicated if need be.
                    This is eg. to process a whole line with only aligned loads.
                    Implemented with additional right border.
  trailingSamples = When reading from a meaningful position, you can read 1 + `trailingSamples` neightbours
                    Their value is NOT guaranteed in any way and is completely undefined.
                    They are guaranteed non-NaN if you've generated borders and did not write into borders.
                    Because of row alignment padding, this is not implemented with a few samples at the end
                    of the buffer, but is instead again a right border extension.

Note: default arguments leads to a gapless representation.
members.type: nothrow @nogc (int w, int h, int border = 0, int rowAlignment = 1, int xMultiplicity = 1, int trailingSamples = 0)

members.name: this
members.comment:
Create from already loaded dense RGBA pixel data.
`buffer` should be allocated with `alignedMalloc`/`alignedRealloc` with an alignment of 1.
Ownership of `buffer` is given to the `OwnedImage`.

members.name: scanlinePtr
members.comment: Returns: A pointer to the pixels at row y. Excluding border pixels.

members.name: scanline
members.comment: Returns: A slice of the pixels at row y. Excluding border pixels.

members.name: size
members.comment:
Resize the image, the content is lost and the new content is undefined.

Params:
  width           = Width of meaningful area in pixels.
  height          = Height of meaningful area in pixels.
  border          = Number of border pixels around the meaningful area. For the right border, this could actually be more depending on other constraints.
  rowAlignment    = Alignment of the _first_ pixel of each row, in bytes (excluding border).
  xMultiplicity   = Starting with the first meaningful pixel of a line, force the number of addressable
                    pixels to be a multiple of `xMultiplicity`.
                    All these "padding" samples added at the right of each line if needed will be considered
                    part of the border and replicated if need be.
                    This is eg. to process a whole line with only aligned loads.
                    Implemented with additional right border.
  trailingSamples = When reading from a meaningful position, you can read 1 + `trailingSamples` neightbours
                    Their value is NOT guaranteed in any way and is completely undefined.
                    They are guaranteed non-NaN if you've generated borders and did not write into borders.
                    Because of row alignment padding, this is not implemented with a few samples at the end
                    of the buffer, but is instead again a right border extension.

Note: Default arguments leads to a gapless representation.
members.type: void(int width, int height, int border = 0, int rowAlignment = 1, int xMultiplicity = 1, int trailingSamples = 0)

members.name: isGapless
members.comment:
Returns: `true` if rows of pixels are immediately consecutive in memory.

members.name: replicateBordersTouching
members.comment: Fill the borders _touching updatedRect_ by taking the nearest existing pixel in the meaningful area.

members.comment: Address of the first meaningful pixel

members.comment: Samples difference between rows of pixels.

members.comment: Address of the allocation itself

name: dplug.graphics.mipmap
comment:
Mipmap pyramid implementation.

Copyright: Guillaume Piolat 2015-2023.
License:   $(LINK2 http://www.boost.org/LICENSE_1_0.txt, Boost License 1.0)

members.comment:
Mipmapped images.
Supports non power-of-two textures.
Size of the i+1-th mipmap is { (width)/2, (height)/2 }
The mipmap owns each of its levels.
members.name: Mipmap
members.constraint: is(COLOR == RGBA) || is(COLOR == L16) || is(COLOR == RGBA16)

members.name: boxAlphaCovIntoPremul
members.comment:
Box-filter, and after such a step the next level is alpha-premultiplied.
This is intended for the first level 0 to level 1 transition, in case of bloom.
This also transitions to linear space to have
more natural highlights (with PBREmissive option)

members.name: this
members.comment: Creates empty

members.name: this
members.comment:
Set number of levels and size
maxLevel = 0 => only one image
maxLevel = 1 => one image + one 2x downsampled mipmap
etc...

This takes ownership of the given image, which is now owned by the `Mipmap`.

members.name: linearMipmapSample
members.comment:
Interpolates a color between mipmap levels.  Floating-point level, spatial linear interpolation.
x and y are in base level coordinates (top-left pixel is on (0.5, 0.5) coordinates).
Clamped to borders.
members.type: nothrow @nogc (float level, float x, float y)

members.name: cubicSample
members.comment:
Cubic filtering mode, using a Catmull-Rom bicubic filter.
Integer level, spatial linear interpolation.
x and y are in base level coordinates (top-left pixel is on (0.5, 0.5) coordinates).
Clamped to borders.
Reference: https://registry.khronos.org/OpenGL/extensions/IMG/IMG_texture_filter_cubic.txt
members.type: nothrow @nogc (int level, float x, float y)

members.name: linearSample
members.comment:
Interpolates a color.  Integer level, spatial linear interpolation.
x and y are in base level coordinates (top-left pixel is on (0.5, 0.5) coordinates).

Resizer ported to D from C. Removed a few features that did'nt make sense in Dplug.
Added Ryhor Spivak work on Lanczos filter... also added a few more lanczos kernels.
Copyright: (c) Guillaume Piolat (2021)

comment:
stb_image_write.h translations
Just the PNG encoder.

members.name: convertImageRefToPNG

The data has to be freed with `free()` or `freeSlice`.

name: dplug.graphics.stb_truetype
comment:
D translation of stb_truetype v0.7 by Sean Barrett
More information on http://nothings.org/stb/stb_truetype.h
Removed:
- texture baking API
- font finding in the TTF itself. Make sure there is only one font in the TTF.

members.name: stbtt_fontinfo
members.comment:
The following structure is defined publically so you can declare one on
the stack or as a global or etc, but you should treat it as opaque.

members.name: stbtt_GetFontOffsetForIndex
members.comment:
Each .ttf/.ttc file may have more than one font. Each font has a sequential
index number starting from 0. Call this function to get the font offset for
a given index; it returns -1 if the index is out of range. A regular .ttf
file will only define one font and it always be at offset 0, so it will
return '0' for index 0, and -1 for all other indices. You can just skip
this step if you know it's that kind of font.

members.name: stbtt_InitFont
members.comment:
Given an offset into the file that defines a font, this function builds
the necessary cached info for the rest of the system. You must allocate
the stbtt_fontinfo yourself, and stbtt_InitFont will fill it out.

members.name: stbtt_FindGlyphIndex
members.comment:
If you're going to perform multiple operations on the same character
and you want a speed-up, call this function with the character you're
going to process, then use glyph-based functions instead of the
codepoint-based functions.

members.name: stbtt_GetCodepointShape
members.comment:
Returns: Number of vertices and fills *vertices with the pointer to them.
         These are expressed in "unscaled" coordinates.
members.originalType: nothrow @nogc int(const stbtt_fontinfo* info, int unicode_codepoint, stbtt_vertex** vertices)

members.comment:
Returns: Number of vertices and fills *vertices with the pointer to them.

members.name: stbtt_GetGlyphHMetrics
members.originalType: nothrow @nogc void(const stbtt_fontinfo* info, int glyph_index, int* advanceWidth, int* leftSideBearing)

members.name: stbtt_GetCodepointKernAdvance
members.comment:
an additional amount to add to the 'advance' value between ch1 and ch2
@TODO; for now always returns 0!
members.originalType: nothrow @nogc int(const stbtt_fontinfo* info, int ch1, int ch2)

members.name: stbtt_GetCodepointHMetrics
members.comment:
leftSideBearing is the offset from the current horizontal position to the left edge of the character
advanceWidth is the offset from the current horizontal position to the next horizontal position
  these are expressed in unscaled coordinates
members.originalType: nothrow @nogc void(const stbtt_fontinfo* info, int codepoint, int* advanceWidth, int* leftSideBearing)

members.name: stbtt_GetFontVMetrics
members.comment:
Ascent is the coordinate above the baseline the font extends; descent
is the coordinate below the baseline the font extends (i.e. it is typically negative)
lineGap is the spacing between one row's descent and the next row's ascent...
so you should advance the vertical position by "*ascent - *descent + *lineGap"
  these are expressed in unscaled coordinates, so you must multiply by
  the scale factor for a given size
members.originalType: nothrow @nogc void(const stbtt_fontinfo* info, int* ascent, int* descent, int* lineGap)

members.name: stbtt_GetFontBoundingBox
members.comment: the bounding box around all possible characters
members.originalType: nothrow @nogc void(const stbtt_fontinfo* info, int* x0, int* y0, int* x1, int* y1)

members.name: stbtt_ScaleForPixelHeight
members.comment:
Computes a scale factor to produce a font whose "height" is 'pixels' tall.
Height is measured as the distance from the highest ascender to the lowest
descender; in other words, it's equivalent to calling stbtt_GetFontVMetrics
and computing:
      scale = pixels / (ascent - descent)
so if you prefer to measure height by the ascent only, use a similar calculation.
members.originalType: nothrow @nogc float(const stbtt_fontinfo* info, float height)

members.name: stbtt_ScaleForMappingEmToPixels
members.comment:
computes a scale factor to produce a font whose EM size is mapped to
'pixels' tall. This is probably what traditional APIs compute, but
I'm not positive.
members.originalType: nothrow @nogc float(const stbtt_fontinfo* info, float pixels)

members.name: stbtt_FreeShape
members.originalType: nothrow @nogc void(const stbtt_fontinfo* info, stbtt_vertex* v)

members.name: stbtt_GetGlyphBitmapBoxSubpixel
members.originalType: nothrow @nogc void(const stbtt_fontinfo* font, int glyph, float scale_x, float scale_y, float shift_x, float shift_y, int* ix0, int* iy0, int* ix1, int* iy1)

members.name: stbtt_GetGlyphBitmapBox
members.originalType: nothrow @nogc void(const stbtt_fontinfo* font, int glyph, float scale_x, float scale_y, int* ix0, int* iy0, int* ix1, int* iy1)

members.name: stbtt_GetCodepointBitmapBoxSubpixel
members.comment:
Same as stbtt_GetCodepointBitmapBox, but you can specify a subpixel
shift for the character.
members.originalType: nothrow @nogc void(const stbtt_fontinfo* font, int codepoint, float scale_x, float scale_y, float shift_x, float shift_y, int* ix0, int* iy0, int* ix1, int* iy1)

members.name: stbtt_GetCodepointBitmapBox
members.comment:
Gets the bbox of the bitmap centered around the glyph origin; so the
bitmap width is ix1-ix0, height is iy1-iy0, and location to place
the bitmap top left is (leftSideBearing*scale,iy0).
(Note that the bitmap uses y-increases-down, but the shape uses
y-increases-up, so CodepointBitmapBox and CodepointBox are inverted.)
members.originalType: nothrow @nogc void(const stbtt_fontinfo* font, int codepoint, float scale_x, float scale_y, int* ix0, int* iy0, int* ix1, int* iy1)

members.name: stbtt_FreeBitmap
members.comment: Frees the allocated bitmap.

members.name: stbtt_MakeCodepointBitmapSubpixel
members.comment:
Same as stbtt_MakeCodepointBitmap, but you can specify a subpixel
shift for the character.

name: rectlist
comment:
A library for integer coord rectangles, and list of those.
    In UI and rendering, useful for caching computation.
    Also port of stb's stretchy buffers.

members.name: rect_t
members.comment: 2D rectangle, int coordinates.

members.name: rectWithCoords
members.comment: Build rectangles with coordinates.

members.name: rectWithSize
members.comment: Build rectangles with one point + dimensions.

members.name: rectWidth
members.comment: Width of rectangle.

members.name: rectHeight
members.comment: Height of rectangle.

members.name: rectIsEmpty
members.comment: Returns: `true` if empty (one or both dimension is zero).

members.name: rectIsSorted
members.comment: Returns: `true` if rectangle has >=0 width and height.

members.name: rectContainsPoint
members.comment: Returns: `true` if it contains a 2D point.

members.name: rectContainsRect
members.comment: Returns: `true` if rectangle `r` contains another `o`.

members.name: rectIntersection
members.comment:
Returns: Intersection of two rectangles.
    Note: check with `rectIsEmpty`.

members.name: rectCheckIntersects
members.comment: Returns: `true` if two rectangles intersect.

members.name: rectMerge
members.comment: Returns a rectangle that encloses both `a` and `b`.

members.name: rectMergeWithPoint
members.comment: Returns: Rectangle that encloses both `a` and point `x`,`y`.

members.name: rectHaveNoOverlap
members.comment:
Returns: `true` if none of the rectangles overlap with each
    other. VERY INEFFICIENT, keep it for debug purpose.

members.name: rectTranslate
members.comment: Returns: A translated rectangle by dx, dy.

members.name: rectGrow
members.comment:
Returns: This rectangle, extended on all sides by the given
    amount.

members.name: rectGrowXY
members.comment:
Returns: This rectangle, extended by different amounts
    horizontally and vertically.

members.name: rectlist_t
members.comment: 2D rectangle list, int coordinates.

members.name: rectlistIsEmpty
members.comment: Returns: `true` if rectlist has no rectangle.

members.name: rectlistPush
members.comment: Push back one rectangle at the end of the list.

members.name: rectlistPushIfNotEmpty
members.comment: Push back one rectangle if not empty.

members.name: rectlistFree
members.comment:
Clear rectangles, and release the allocation.
    List is now an empty list.

members.name: rectlistCount
members.comment: Returns: number of rectangle in list.

members.name: rectlistNth
members.comment: Return nth items of list.

members.name: rectlistClear
members.comment: Clear list of rectangles to 0 items, but keep the allocation.

members.name: rectlistRectangles
members.comment: Returns: rectangles in the list as a slice.

members.name: rectlistBounds
members.comment:
Returns: A single rectangle that contains other rectangles in the
    list, as a bounding box.

members.name: rectlistHasNoOverlap
members.comment:
Returns: `true` if none of the rectangles overlap with each
    other. VERY INEFFICIENT, keep it for debug purpose.

members.name: rectlistRemoveOverlapping
members.comment:
Take rectangles in input, make it non-overlapping and push the
    result in output rectlist.
    Warning: Input is destroyed!
    Note: this is not very efficient, O(n^2) complexity.

members.name: rectSubtractionH
members.comment:
Make 4 boxes that are A without C (C is contained in A)
    Some may be empty though since C touch at least one edge of A.

  General case: at least one of D, E, F or G is empty.
    +---------+               +---------+
    |    A    |               |    D    |
    |  +---+  |   After split +--+---+--+
    |  | C |  |        =>     | E|   |F |
    |  +---+  |               +--+---+--+
    |         |               |    G    |
    +---------+               +---------+
Number of items currently in stretchy buffer.

TM_CharData
members.comment:
An individual cell of text-mode buffer.

    Either you access it or use the `print` functions.

    The first four bytes are a Unicode codepoint, conflated
    with a grapheme and font "glyph". There is one font, and
    it's 8x8. Not all codepoints exist in that font.

    Next 4-bit is foreground color in a 16 color palette.
    Next 4-bit is background color in a 16 color palette.

members.name: glyph
members.comment:
Unicode codepoint to represent. This library doesn't
        compose codepoints.

members.name: color
members.comment:
Low nibble = foreground color (0 to 15)
        High nibble = background color (0 to 15)

members.name: style
members.comment:
Style of that character, a combination of `TM_Style`
        flags.

members.name: TM_Style
members.comment: Character styles.

members.name: TM_styleNone
members.comment: no style

members.name: TM_styleShiny
members.comment: <shiny>, emissive light

members.name: TM_styleBold
members.comment: <b> or <strong>, pixels are 2x1

members.name: TM_styleUnder
members.comment: <u>, lowest row is filled

members.name: TM_styleBlink
members.comment: <blink> need call to console.update(dt)

members.name: TM_BoxStyle
members.comment: A box style is composed of 8 glyphs.

members.name: TM_Palette
members.comment:
Predefined palettes (default: vintage is loaded).
    You can either load a predefined palette, or change
    colors individually.

TM_paletteCampbell
TM_paletteOneHalfLight

TM_PALETTE_NUM

Number of pre-defined palettes.
    Keep it sync with above.

members.name: TM_Font
members.comment:
Selected vintage font.
    There is only one font, our goal is to provide a Unicode
    8x8 font suitable for most languages. So other font
    options like 8x14 were removed, though it would be
    possible to add it back in the library.

members.name: TM_font8x8
members.comment:
A font dumped from BIOS around 2003, then extended.
        Since this library evolved to become a single font
        library, it may be fitting to evolve it contrarily
        to history and make it more "entertaining" rather
        than business-IBM-PC style.

halign

members.comment:
If `true`: the output buffer is considered unchanged
between calls.
In this case we can draw less.

members.name: borderShiny
members.comment: Is the border color itself <shiny>?

members.comment: The <blink> time in milliseconds.

members.comment:
Quantity of blur added by TM_shiny / <shiny>
(1.0f means default).

members.name: blurScale
members.comment:
Kernel size in multiple of default value.
This changes the blur filter width.

members.name: blurForeground
members.comment: Whether foreground color contributes to blur.

members.name: blurBackground
members.comment:
Whether background color contributes to blur.
Note that this usually gives surprising results
together with non-black background. Only really
works if everything is shiny.

members.name: noiseTexture
members.comment: Luminance blue noise, applied to blur effect.

members.name: noiseAmount
members.comment: Quantity of that texture (1.0f means default).

members.name: tonemapping
members.comment: Enable or disable tonemapping.

members.name: tonemappingRatio
members.comment:
Channels that exceed 1.0f, bleed that much in
other channels.

members.name: crtEmulation
members.comment:
Quite basic CRT emulation.
Basically work in progress.

members.name: vignetting
members.comment: Is vignetting enabled?

members.name: vignettingCenterX
members.comment:
Location in output buffer.
0 is left, 1 is right.
Default = center.

members.name: vignettingCenterY
members.comment:
Location in output buffer (0 to 1).
0 is top, 1 is bottom.
Default = center.

members.name: vignettingDistance
members.comment:
How far does it take to be completely
of `vignettingColor`.
Default = Twice a half-diagonal of the
output buffer size.

members.name: vignettingOpacity
members.comment: Vignetting opacity, from 0.0 to 1.0

members.name: vignettingColor
members.comment: sRGB 8-bit, vignetting color

size
members.comment:
(MANDATORY)
        Set/get size of text buffer.
        Warning: this clears the screen like calling `cls`.

        See_also: `outbuf`

members.name: size
members.comment: ditto

members.name: suggestedWidth
members.comment:
Given selected font and size of console screen, give
        a suggested output buffer size (in pixels).
        However, this library will manage to render in
        whatever buffer size you give, so this is completely
        optional.

members.name: suggestedHeight
members.comment: ditto

members.comment: Get number of text columns.

members.name: rows
members.comment: Get number of text rows.

members.comment: Set current foreground color.

members.name: bg
members.comment: Set current background color.

members.name: style
members.comment: Set current character attributes aka style.

members.name: save
members.comment:
Save/restore state, that includes:
        - foreground color
        - background color
        - cursor position
        - character style

        Note: This won't report stack errors.
              You MUST pair your save/restore calls, or
              endure eventual display bugs.

members.name: restore
members.comment: ditto

members.name: font
members.comment:
Set/get font selection.
        But well, there is only one font.

members.name: font
members.comment: ditto

members.name: charWidth
members.comment:
Get width/height of a character in selected font.
        Normally you don't need this since the actual size
        in output buffer is different.

members.name: charHeight
members.comment: ditto

members.name: palette
members.comment: Load a palette preset.

members.name: getPaletteName
members.comment:
Get a palette preset name.
        There are 0..TM_PALETTE_NUM palette presets.

members.name: setPaletteEntry
members.comment:
Set/get palette entries.

        Params: entry Palette index, must be 0 to 15
                r Red value.
                g Green value.
                b Blue value.
                a Alpha value.
        All values are clamped to [0 to 255].

        Note: unlike a real text mode, colors can be
              transparent. Actually color 0 is transparent
              by default, so that it can be blit by default
              over a frame.

members.name: setPaletteEntry
members.comment: ditto

members.comment: ditto

members.name: findColorMatch
members.comment:
find best match for color `r`, `g`, `b` in palette.

        Returns: A palette index to use with `fg` or `bg`.

members.name: options
members.comment:
Set other options.
        Those control important rendering options, and
        changing those tend to redraw the whole buffer.

members.name: characters
members.comment:
Access character buffer directly.
        Returns: Consecutive char data, columns x rows.
                 Characters are stored in row-major order.

members.name: print
members.comment:
Print text to console at current cursor position.
        Text input MUST be UTF-8 or Unicode codepoint.

        See_also: `render()`

members.comment: ditto

members.name: print
members.comment: ditto

members.name: print
members.comment: ditto

members.name: newline
members.comment: ditto

members.name: cls
members.comment: `cls` clears the screen, filling it with spaces.

members.name: clearScreen
members.comment: ditto

members.name: locate
members.comment:
Change text cursor position. -1 indicate "keep".
        Do nothing for each dimension separately, if
        position is out of bounds.

members.name: column
members.comment: ditto

members.name: row
members.comment: ditto

members.name: cprint
members.comment:
Print text to console at current cursor position,
        encoded in the CCL language (much the same as in
        console-colors DUB package).
        Text input MUST be UTF-8.

        Accepted tags:
        - <COLORNAME> such as:
          <black> <red>      <green>   <orange>
          <blue>  <magenta>  <cyan>    <lgrey>
          <grey>  <lred>     <lgreen>  <yellow>
          <lblue> <lmagenta> <lcyan>   <white>

        each corresponding to color 0 to 15 in the palette.

        Unknown tags have no effect and are removed.
        Tags CAN'T have attributes.
        Here, CCL is modified (vs console-colors) to be
        ALWAYS VALID.

        - STYLE tags, such as:
        <strong>, <b>, <u>, <blink>, <shiny>

        Escaping:
        - To pass '<' as text and not a tag, use <
        - To pass '>' as text and not a tag, use >
        - To pass '&' as text not an entity, use &

        See_also: `print`

members.name: cprintln
members.comment: ditto

members.name: printANS
members.comment:
Print while interpreting ANSI codes.
        This is designed to print UTF-8 encoded .ans files
        directly, who support more characters.

        That .ans image is displayed in current cursor
        position as a kind of bitmap, so it's blit without
        line feeds or scrolling.

        Doesn't change cursor position.

members.name: printANS_CP437
members.comment:
Print while interpreting CP437-encoded ANSI codes.
        This is designed to print CP437 encoded .ans files
        directly, such as displayed on website:
            https://16colo.rs/

        That .ans image is displayed in current cursor
        position as a kind of bitmap, so it's blit without
        line feeds or scrolling.

        Doesn't change cursor position.

members.name: printXP
members.comment:
Print a .xp binary file from REXPaint.

        That .xp image is blit in current cursor position as
        a bitmap. Doesn't change cursor position.

        It is advantageous since .xp has up to 9 layers and
        is gzipped.

        Params:
             xpBytes   = The .xp file contents.
             layerMask = What layers to draw, -1 means all.
                         Bit 0 for layer 1
                         etc...
                         Bit 8 for layer 9.

members.name: printImage
members.comment:
Print a `TM_Image` in the console buffer.
        At cursor position.
        This is meant to mirror the behaviour of `printXP`:
          - it overwrite the style with current style
          - it changes the current color
          - it preserved cursor position

members.name: fillRect
members.comment:
Fill a rectangle with a character, using the current
        foreground, background, and style.

        This doesn't use, nor change, cursor position.

members.name: box
members.comment:
Draw a box with a particular box style.

        This doesn't use, nor change, cursor position.
members.originalType: pure nothrow @nogc @safe void(int x, int y, int w, int h, TM_BoxStyle bs)

members.name: outbuf
members.originalType: nothrow @nogc @system void(void* pixels, int width, int height, ptrdiff_t pitchBytes)

members.name: render
members.comment:
(MANDATORY, well if you want some output)

        Render console to output buffer. After this call,
        the output buffer is up-to-date with the changes in
        text buffer content.

        Depending on the options, only the rectangle in
        `getUpdateRect()` will get updated.

members.name: update
members.comment:
Make time progress, so that <blink> does blink.
        Give it your frame's delta time, in seconds.

members.name: hasPendingUpdate
members.comment:
Return if there is pending updates to draw.

        This answer is only valid until the next `render()`.
        Also invalidated if you print, change style,
        palette, options, etc.

members.name: getUpdateRect
members.comment:
Returns the outbuf rectangle which is going to be
        updated when `render()` is called.

        This is expressed in output buffer coordinates.

        This answer is only valid until the next `render()`
        call. Also invalidated if you print, change style,
        palette, or options, etc.

        Note: In case of nothing to redraw, it's width and
              height will be zero.

members.name: hit
members.comment:
Convert pixel position to character position.
        Which character location is at pixel x,y ?

        Returns: `true` if a character is pointed at, else
                 `false`.
                 If no hit, `*col` and `*row` are left
                 unchanged.

members.name: hit
members.comment: ditto
